# Local council approves new bike lane network downtown

By Jordan Reyes · Published June 12, 2026 · 4 min read

The city council voted 7-2 on Tuesday to approve a $4.2 million plan to build
protected bike lanes across the downtown core, a project supporters say will
make commuting safer and opponents argue will worsen traffic congestion.

The plan, which has been debated for nearly two years, will add roughly twelve
miles of separated cycling infrastructure over the next three years. Council
member Dana Whitfield called it "a generational investment in how people move
through our city."

## What happens next

Construction is expected to begin in the fall, starting with the Main Street
corridor. Residents can submit feedback through the city's online portal until
the end of the month.
